003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet;
018
019import static jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse.*;
020import static java.util.logging.Level.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
024
025import java.io.*;
026import java.text.*;
027import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
028import java.util.function.*;
029import java.util.logging.*;
030
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.commons.logging.Logger;
033import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.http.response.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
038
039import jakarta.servlet.*;
040import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
041
042/**
043 * Servlet implementation of a REST resource.
044 *
045 * <p>
046 *    The {@link RestServlet} class is the entry point for your REST resources.
047 *    It extends directly from <l>HttpServlet</l> and is deployed like any other servlet.
048 * </p>
049 * <p>
050 *    When the servlet <l>init()</l> method is called, it triggers the code to find and process the <l>@Rest</l>
051 *    annotations on that class and all child classes.
052 *    These get constructed into a {@link RestContext} object that holds all the configuration
053 *    information about your resource in a read-only object.
054 * </p>
055 *
056 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
057 *    <li class='note'>
058 *       Users will typically extend from {@link BasicRestServlet} or {@link BasicRestServletGroup}
059 *       instead of this class directly.
060 * </ul>
061 *
062 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
063 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestAnnotatedClassBasics">@Rest-Annotated Class Basics</a>
064 * </ul>
065 *
066 * @serial exclude
067 */
068@SuppressWarnings({
069   "java:S115" // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., MSG_servletInitError)
070})
071public abstract class RestServlet extends HttpServlet {
072
073   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
074   private static final AnnotationProvider AP = AnnotationProvider.INSTANCE;
075
076   // Error message constants
077   private static final String MSG_servletInitError = "Servlet init error on class ''{0}''";
078
079   private final AtomicReference<RestContext> context = new AtomicReference<>();
080   private final AtomicReference<Exception> initException = new AtomicReference<>();
081
082   /**
083    * The programmatic configuration builder stashed on this instance, or <jk>null</jk> when the
084    * resource was constructed without a builder.  Mutable so it can be written by either the
085    * {@link #RestServlet(RestBuilder)} constructor or {@link Builder#build()} (the no-arg-only setter-stash path).
086    * Read non-reflectively by {@link RestContext} during construction so builder-supplied values take precedence
087    * over {@link Rest @Rest} annotation values.
088    */
089   @SuppressWarnings({
090      "java:S2226" // Write-once-before-service stash: set only by the (RestBuilder) constructor or Builder.build() (the no-arg + setter-stash path) before the servlet is placed in service, then read-only; it cannot be final because of the no-arg construction path.
091   })
092   transient RestBuilder<?> restBuilder;
093
094   /**
095    * Default constructor.
096    */
097   protected RestServlet() {}
098
099   /**
100    * Builder-injection constructor.
101    *
102    * @param builder The programmatic configuration builder.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
103    */
104   protected RestServlet(RestBuilder<?> builder) {
105      this.restBuilder = builder;
106   }
107
108   /**
109    * Returns the programmatic configuration builder stashed on this resource, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
110    *
111    * @return The stashed builder, or <jk>null</jk>.
112    * @since 10.0.0
113    */
114   @SuppressWarnings({
115      "java:S1452" // RestBuilder<?> wildcard return intentional; concrete builder type varies by REST resource class
116   })
117   public RestBuilder<?> getRestBuilder() {
118      return restBuilder;
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Creates a new fluent builder for programmatically configuring an instance of the specified resource type.
123    *
124    * <p>
125    * Builder-supplied values take precedence over the resource class's own {@link Rest @Rest} annotation values.
126    *
127    * @param <R> The resource type.
128    * @param type The resource type to build.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
129    * @return A new builder.
130    * @since 10.0.0
131    */
132   public static <R extends RestServlet> DefaultBuilder<R> builder(Class<R> type) {
133      return new DefaultBuilder<>(type);
134   }
135
136   /**
137    * Returns the resource-level authenticator for this servlet, or <jk>null</jk> to fall through to the
138    * {@code @Rest(authenticator=...)} annotation / registered bean / {@code @Bean} factory method.
139    *
140    * <p>
141    * This is the lowest-precedence rung in the {@link RestAuthenticator} resolution chain (see design spec
142    * &sect;5.3).  Subclasses can override it to supply an authenticator programmatically.
143    *
144    * @param beanStore The bean store for dependency resolution.
145    * @return The authenticator, or <jk>null</jk> (the default).
146    * @since 10.0.0
147    */
148   public RestAuthenticator createAuthenticator(BeanStore beanStore) {
149      return null;
150   }
151
152   @Override /* Overridden from GenericServlet */
153   public synchronized void destroy() {
154      var c = context.getAndSet(null);
155      if (nn(c))
156         c.destroy();
157      super.destroy();
158   }
159
160   /**
161    * Returns the read-only context object that contains all the configuration information about this resource.
162    *
163    * <p>
164    * This object is <jk>null</jk> during the call to {@link #init(ServletConfig)} but is populated by the time
165    * {@link #init()} is called.
166    *
167    * <p>
168    * Resource classes that don't extend from {@link RestServlet} can add the following method to their class to get
169    * access to this context object:
170    * <p class='bjava'>
171    *    <jk>public void</jk> init(RestServletContext <jv>context</jv>) <jk>throws</jk> Exception;
172    * </p>
173    *
174    * @return The context information on this servlet.
175    */
176   public synchronized RestContext getContext() {
177      var rc = context.get();
178      if (rc == null)
179         throw new InternalServerError("RestContext object not set on resource.");
180      return rc;
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Returns the path for this resource as defined by the @Rest(path) annotation or RestContext.Builder.path(String) method
185    * concatenated with those on all parent classes.
186    *
187    * @return The path defined on this servlet, or an empty string if not specified.
188    */
189   public synchronized String getPath() {
190      var context2 = this.context.get();
191      if (nn(context2))
192         return context2.getFullPath();
193      var ci = ClassInfo.of(getClass());
194      // @formatter:off
195      return rstream(AP.find(Rest.class, ci))
196         .map(x -> x.inner().path())
197         .filter(Utils::ne)
198         .map(StringUtils::trimSlashes)
199         .findFirst()
200         .orElse("");
201      // @formatter:on
202   }
203
204   /**
205    * Returns the runtime-overridden top-level mount paths for this resource.
206    *
207    * <p>
208    * Subclasses can override this method to substitute the {@link Rest#paths() @Rest(paths)} annotation
209    * defaults at construction time. This is the &quot;getter&quot; rung in the runtime-override resolution
210    * chain documented on {@link RestContext#getPaths()}:
211    * <ol>
212    *    <li>Programmatic ({@code RestContext.Builder.paths(String...)}) &mdash; highest precedence.
213    *    <li>This {@code getPaths()} getter (when non-{@code null}).
214    *    <li>{@code @Rest(paths={...})} annotation default (SVL-resolved per element, comma-split) &mdash;
215    *       lowest precedence.
216    * </ol>
217    *
218    * <h5 class='section'>Accepted return shapes:</h5><ul>
219    *    <li>{@code null} &mdash; no override; resolution falls through to the {@code @Rest(paths=...)}
220    *       annotation default.
221    *    <li>{@link String} &mdash; a single path or a comma-delimited list, e.g.
222    *       {@code "/healthz,/readyz"}.
223    *    <li>{@code String[]} &mdash; each element may itself be a comma-delimited list.
224    *    <li>{@link java.util.Collection Collection}, {@link java.util.List List},
225    *       {@link java.util.Set Set}, {@link Iterable}, or {@link java.util.stream.Stream Stream}
226    *       of any of the above (nested mixes are recursively flattened).
227    *    <li>Primitive arrays (boxed during flattening).
228    * </ul>
229    *
230    * <p>
231    * Non-{@link String} leaves are coerced via {@link String#valueOf(Object)}.  Each leaf string then runs
232    * through the same per-element pipeline used for {@code @Rest(paths=...)} annotation elements: SVL is
233    * applied via the bean-store-backed {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.svl.VarResolverSession VarResolverSession}
234    * (so {@code $C{key}} consults the live {@link org.apache.juneau.config.Config Config}, {@code $E{NAME,default}}
235    * and {@code $S{prop,default}} resolve through the bootstrap variable catalog), and the post-SVL value is
236    * comma-split with each piece trimmed and empty pieces dropped.
237    *
238    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
239    *    <li class='note'>
240    *       Returning {@code null} (the default) means &quot;inherit annotation&quot; &mdash; the next rung resolves.
241    *    <li class='note'>
242    *       Returning {@code new String[0]}, an empty collection, an empty string, or a blank string means
243    *       &quot;explicitly clear&quot; &mdash; no top-level mounts.
244    *    <li class='note'>
245    *       The scalar accessor {@link #getPath()} remains orthogonal to this method &mdash; the singular
246    *       {@code @Rest(path=...)} attribute has its own resolution chain and is unaffected.
247    * </ul>
248    *
249    * @return The runtime-overridden mount paths in any accepted shape, or {@code null} to inherit the
250    *    annotation default.
251    * @since 10.0.0
252    */
253   public Object getPaths() { return null; }
254
255   /**
256    * Returns the current thread-local HTTP request.
257    *
258    * @return The current thread-local HTTP request, or <jk>null</jk> if it wasn't created.
259    */
260   public synchronized RestRequest getRequest() { return getContext().getLocalSession().getOpSession().getRequest(); }
261
262   /**
263    * Returns the current thread-local HTTP response.
264    *
265    * @return The current thread-local HTTP response, or <jk>null</jk> if it wasn't created.
266    */
267   public synchronized RestResponse getResponse() { return getContext().getLocalSession().getOpSession().getResponse(); }
268
269   @Override /* Overridden from Servlet */
270   public synchronized void init(ServletConfig servletConfig) throws ServletException {
271      try {
272         if (nn(context.get()))
273            return;
274         super.init(servletConfig);
275         context.set(new RestContext(new RestContext.Args(this.getClass(), null, servletConfig, () -> this, "", null, null, null, RestContext.ContextKind.ROOT, restBuilder)));
276         context.get().postInit();
277         context.get().postInitChildFirst();
278      } catch (ServletException e) {
279         initException.set(e);
280         log(SEVERE, e, MSG_servletInitError, cn(this));
281         throw e;
282      } catch (BasicHttpException e) {
283         initException.set(e);
284         log(SEVERE, e, MSG_servletInitError, cn(this));
285      } catch (Exception e) {
286         initException.set(new InternalServerError(e));
287         log(SEVERE, e, MSG_servletInitError, cn(this));
288      }
289   }
290
291   /**
292    * Log a message.
293    *
294    * <p>
295    * Subclasses can intercept the handling of these messages by overriding {@link #doLog(Level, Throwable, Supplier)}.
296    *
297    * @param level The log level.
298    * @param msg The message to log.
299    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
300    */
301   public void log(Level level, String msg, Object...args) {
302      doLog(level, null, fs(msg, args));
303   }
304
305   /**
306    * Log a message.
307    *
308    * <p>
309    * Subclasses can intercept the handling of these messages by overriding {@link #doLog(Level, Throwable, Supplier)}.
310    *
311    * @param level The log level.
312    * @param cause The cause.
313    * @param msg The message to log.
314    * @param args Optional {@link MessageFormat}-style arguments.
315    */
316   public void log(Level level, Throwable cause, String msg, Object...args) {
317      doLog(level, cause, fs(msg, args));
318   }
319
320   /**
321    * The main service method.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Subclasses can optionally override this method if they want to tailor the behavior of requests.
325    */
326   @Override /* Overridden from Servlet */
327   public void service(HttpServletRequest r1, HttpServletResponse r2) throws ServletException, InternalServerError, IOException {
328      try {
329         if (nn(initException.get()))
330            throw initException.get();
331         if (context.get() == null)
332            throw new InternalServerError(
333               "Servlet {0} not initialized.  init(ServletConfig) was not called.  This can occur if you've overridden this method but didn't call super.init(RestConfig).", cn(this));
334         getContext().execute(this, r1, r2);
335
336      } catch (Exception e) {
337         r2.sendError(SC_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, lm(e));
338      }
339   }
340
341   /**
342    * Main logger method.
343    *
344    * <p>
345    * The default behavior logs a message to the Java logger of the class name.
346    *
347    * <p>
348    * Subclasses can override this method to implement their own logger handling.
349    *
350    * @param level The log level.
351    * @param cause Optional throwable.
352    * @param msg The message to log.
353    */
354   protected void doLog(Level level, Throwable cause, Supplier<String> msg) {
355      var c = context.get();
356      var logger = c == null ? null : c.getLogger();
357      if (logger == null)
358         logger = Logger.getLogger(cn(this));
359      logger.log(level, cause, msg);
360   }
361
362   /**
363    * Sets the context object for this servlet.
364    *
365    * <p>
366    * This method is effectively a no-op if {@link #init(ServletConfig)} has already been called.
367    *
368    * @param context Sets the context object on this servlet.
369    * @throws ServletException If error occurred during initialization.
370    */
371   protected synchronized void setContext(RestContext context) throws ServletException {
372      if (this.context.get() == null) {
373         super.init(context.getBuilder());
374         this.context.set(context);
375      }
376   }
377
378   /**
379    * Fluent builder for programmatically configuring a {@link RestServlet} subclass.
380    *
381    * <p>
382    * This is the subclassable, self-typed (CRTP) flavor builder.  Its {@code SELF} type parameter is left open so
383    * a user subclass's bespoke setters chain with true covariant returns alongside the inherited
384    * {@link RestBuilder} surface.  For the common case where the builder is not subclassed,
385    * use {@link RestServlet#builder(Class)} which returns the concrete {@link DefaultBuilder} leaf.
386    *
387    * @param <R> The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.
388    * @param <SELF> The concrete builder type (self type).
389    * @since 10.0.0
390    */
391   @SuppressWarnings({
392      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
393   })
394   public static class Builder<R extends RestServlet, SELF extends Builder<R, SELF>> extends AbstractRestBuilder<R, SELF> {
395
396      /**
397       * Constructor.
398       *
399       * @param type The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
400       */
401      protected Builder(Class<R> type) {
402         super(type);
403      }
404
405      @Override /* AbstractRestBuilder */
406      public R build() {
407         var r = createResource();
408         r.restBuilder = this;
409         return r;
410      }
411   }
412
413   /**
414    * Concrete default leaf builder returned by {@link RestServlet#builder(Class)} for the common (non-subclassed)
415    * case, so callers are not forced to spell the {@code SELF} type parameter.
416    *
417    * @param <R> The resource type produced by {@link #build()}.
418    * @since 10.0.0
419    */
420   public static final class DefaultBuilder<R extends RestServlet> extends Builder<R, DefaultBuilder<R>> {
421      DefaultBuilder(Class<R> type) {
422         super(type);
423      }
424   }
425}
